review_body_text: - Daily job may be repetitive (depends on the team you are on) - Moderate workload (depends on the team you are on) - Classic management style - Healthy working environment Recommended for freshers

review_title: Great bonus and increment. But old system
review_body_text: Good for new graduates to start their career in banking as you tend to do a lot of things. The increment is above average and bonuses are decent. This comes with old system and SOPs as they prefer to reduce cost. A lot of paperwork to be done. WLB depends on your department
Can say very stressful heavy workload, but friendly people and good pay, will help me when i ask for help, very traditional and corporate work culture

The Hire Purchase Department at Public Bank provides a structured and professional working environment that offers valuable exposure to consumer financing and credit operations. The department is well-organized, with clear procedures for loan processing, credit assessment, documentation, and compliance with banking regulations. This allows staff and trainees to understand the full hire purchase financing cycle, particularly for vehicle loans.
The working culture is very structured and hierarchy. Multiple reporting layers. Hardly have employee empowerment. Minimal training and development programmes.

My experience working here has been largely positive, with a supportive environment that encourages learning and growth. The team is friendly, approachable, and always willing to help when needed. I appreciate the collaborative culture, where ideas are welcomed and everyone’s input is valued. The management team is generally understanding and open to feedback, creating a comfortable space to discuss challenges and improvements. The workplace also provides good opportunities to develop new skills, whether through hands-on tasks or guidance from more experienced colleagues. The work processes are quite organized, which helps in managing tasks efficiently. While there is still room for improvement in terms of workload balance and clearer communication at times, the overall atmosphere remains motivating.
Good salary and bonuses Alot of annual leaves Average for benefits Stress depends on department Good environment and can learn alot Overall good for starting career here

review_title: Very heavy workloads, no work life balance & toxic management
review_body_text: - I always saw my colleagues were always working during their annual leaves and weekends in the office for the whole day due to strict deadlines set by ISO regulations - My mentor even asked me to cancel my annual leaves to complete my workloads, and my mentor asked me to complete my workloads during my annual leaves, weekends and public holiday despite unrealistic goals set by the boss and the team. - Your workload will be increased without letting you know in advance or without asking your permission to do so. - Your colleagues will punish you with extra workloads if you didn't complete their KPI (number of cases to be completed per day) - Even if you have a little progress within 4 months, the boss and my mentor won't be happy or celebrate your progress, the only thing they do is by forcing you to do faster with increasing amount of workloads. - My mentor has very biased treatment, my mentor is always friendly towards the other colleagues (who are close to my mentor), but when comes to my male colleague (in same department) and me as a new worker, my mentor always give very bad compliment or judge us in a biased way. My mentor also reacted excessively on trivial things about me and controlled me on every movement ("Don't show that you are not happy, because I saw you that you banged the table with documents", while the actual event: The documents are too heavy that I can't carry properly, hence I have to arrange the documents with accidental bang). My mentor also talked bad about male colleague behind his back when male colleague took MC due to his illness.
cons_text: No work life balance, toxic & poor management

review_title: Forget about life if u work in this company
review_body_text: Place to look for experience only (1 - 2 years cap), dont mind if u being diaturb at weekend especially hire purchase dept. long working hours n no wfh

Benefits:- Basic salary meets the market range. However, increment, year-end bonus and performance bonus are based on your job performance and supervisor. Management Style:- Old-school management required multiple layers to apply work access. No flexible working hours and no remote. Promotion will be mostly based on the service years rather than job performance.

review_title: Good Company but not a Good Division
review_body_text: Great benefits, encouraging bonus and competitive salary if you work in HQ. Work life balance is achievable if you're in the right division, right department and under the right team. One step wrong, crazy amount of OT and gaslighting. Could be toxic and unbearable.

review_title: Monetary benefits is fine but overload with work
review_body_text: Public Bank offers strong pay, bonuses & stability, but suffers from outdated systems, clunky app, heavy workload & limited work‑life balance. Turnover rate is quite high
- Boss treats workers like highschoolers - Little to no staff engagement - Require staffs to stay back but wont pay for overtime - Environment limits you from learning new stuff
Good bonus and benefit. For career growth depends on department and bosses. They easily hire fresh grad. No work life balance and they will pressure you all the time
Traditional type of work experience. Good place to learn for fresh. Typical corporate company with a lot of procedures and ethics that you need to follow.

What is the work environment and culture like at the company? The company has a fast-paced and high-performance work culture that challenges you to grow. While expectations are high, it encourages resilience and strong work ethics. It’s a place where dedication and hard work are key to success. What is a typical day like for you at the company? A typical day is dynamic and fast-moving, with everyone focused and efficient. There's always something happening, which keeps the work engaging and helps sharpen multitasking and time management skills
Intense pressure & high expectations. Very Chinese man and traditional way of approaching work. Expected to OT everday and even on weekends without getting paid OT.

I am currently working there as a Management Trainee, i find it even in a week it is too overwhelming. The management is on another whole different level, they tend to stick with the paper based era whereas people are going towards technologies. I would say run while you can, there is no good employee welfare, pantry is provided but not with food, works are expected to be learnt in one go despite learning phase. Superiors are just not very welcoming, there is this heavy aura around them. Working here means forgetting of going back on time, the very first day itself the manager told me " here i don't want people to go back on time" usual working time is from 08:45 am to 5:45pm but you are required to work more but none is claimable. Basically speaking no one goes back on time sharp 05:45pm. Overall the management and the way they handle the process of everything is super old school, they still have typewriters to write on vouchers. therefore, explore other firm or field this place is not recommended for the sake of sanity.

Didnt work here, but got the chances to be interviewed but unfortunately, it didnt goes really well. The interview process was nice tho, the hr seems professional. They call u right after seeing ur resume

Pros: Bonus and increment consistently high every year medical benefits great teammates, almost no politics Cons: 1 person would do jobs of 3 people at other banks Expected to learn on the go old tech and equipment

review_title: A good company to start with in banking line
review_body_text: Working at Public Bank Berhad can be a rewarding yet demanding experience, especially in the fast-paced environment of a commercial bank. Like most banks, the workload can be overwhelming, with employees often expected to handle multiple responsibilities, tight deadlines, and high-performance targets. Long working hours are common, particularly in departments such as sales, credit analysis, and customer service. However, this is considered the norm in the banking industry, where efficiency and accuracy are crucial. One of the key factors that can make the experience more enjoyable is having supportive and competent teammates. A good team can help ease the workload, provide guidance, and create a positive work environment, making it easier to manage stress. Public Bank is known for its structured work culture, and employees who can adapt to its disciplined and customer-focused approach tend to succeed. Overall, while the job can be challenging, it offers stability, career growth opportunities, and valuable banking experience. Employees who thrive under pressure and enjoy teamwork may find Public Bank a good place to build a long-term career.

not recommended for those who didnt know handle their stress. they can be crazy to meet up their kpi. those fresh graduate who want to work here need to have strong physical and mental to sustain in a long run.
Good pay and bonus. Team members also nice . But in sale, have to deal with a lot of pressure. Everything is manual and use a lot of paper. Good for fresh graduate to take experience
Bonus and increment are definitely the best in the market. Workload OK depending on the team and bosses. Turn based style promotions. Annual leaves and Mc good

review_title: Benefit wise and working like servant.
review_body_text: What is the best part of working at the company? The remunerations given are very beneficial especially for holiday leave and medical benefit. What is the most stressful part about working at the company? Unreasonable workload. No work-life balance. You have to give full commitment to this organisation even in weekend especially when you are involve in marketing scope. What is the work environment and culture like at the company? Work like dog, work until you die, no one gonna appreciate you. What is a typical day like for you at the company? It was hectic working. Everyone walking like super flash, too much work to be settle.
Benefit: Intern can learn a lot of knowledge and skills here. The salary pay on time. Challenge: The working is quite stressful. Need to adopt quickly in working

Public sector banks are known for offering stable and attractive salary packages, making them a popular choice for individuals seeking long-term career opportunities. While the salary structure varies based on the position and level of experience, public bank employees typically enjoy competitive pay scales that are often revised based on recommendations from government pay commissions. A key advantage is the inclusion of various allowances such as housing rent allowance (HRA), dearness allowance (DA), and special allowances. In addition to the fixed salary, public bank employees also receive additional perks like medical benefits, pension schemes, and paid leave. Bonuses and annual increments are also part of the compensation structure, making it financially rewarding over time. The salary in public banks may not be as high as in private sector banks initially, but the long-term financial security, work-life balance, and other benefits make public banks a desirable option for many. Additionally, public bank employees often have more structured career paths, including promotional avenues that lead to higher pay grades and responsibilities.

review_title: Bad corporate culture with good benefits
review_body_text: Last time staff needed to perform for annual dinners if selected. No option for you to reject. I heard it is different now but despised the cult-like annual dinners. Good thing are the benefits like AL and bonus.

review_title: KPI too high, but benefit all great
review_body_text: Branch environment is good, got to know and work together with new people. Benefit and salary also good, but the kpi is too high for even for staff thats in under probation.
No worklife balance, prepare to work overtime without extra pay. Management is toxic, they do not care about your concerns. It was a very bad experience.
pb has a lot of benefits. good for fresh grad that wants to pursue career in financial sector. you'll learn more about it here at pb, staff are friendly

review_title: Great benefit but no work life balance
review_body_text: Cannot go back on time, a lot of manual work and unnecessary things. Great benefits such as annual leave, medical benefit. Should focus on tech such as fill up form online instead of using paper

A good starting point for fresh graduate to start their career. The starting salary is quite good. However, the manangement keeps pushing figure which could make people feel stressful and also almost ot everyday.
if you like to work late at night without ot, getting scold with your senior, getting push by your colleges, unproper training given by your company, then this is the job your looking for.

review_title: Fast paced and have to be independent
review_body_text: They'll teach you the basics, provided training and little knowledge. You need to stand on your two feet and learn as you go. Once you get the hang of it, things might be easier but always watch out for your target (KPI)
A day in the Bank can be busy depending on the the day or the weekend. The week before any celebrations can be busy . Supportive colleagues around to show and guide how the job is done .

What is the best part of working at the company? Good employee benefit. Performance bonus , commission scheme for sales staff , retirement benefits for all officer level .EPF benefits. What is the most stressful part about working at the company? For sales for sure is sales target.the only things is difficult to get reffaller for small branches What is the work environment and culture like at the company? Good and friendly staff , senior staff more helpful What is a typical day like for you at the company? When MCO take is the typical time for me

No work life balance, expected to work 13 hours everyday, high workload and marketing officer will keep pressuring you to meet their figure, supposed to work as a team but end up each of team member only work for their own target and goal, not recommend

review_title: Helpful senior but seniority culture quite strong
review_body_text: Pbb is good to learn various things. However, the workflow very messy & u gotta do everything & not specialised in something, which not align with my career goals. System very outdated & bad. Need to relief ppl a lot if u are from branch.

Pros - The renumeration packages and bonuses are great. There are fixed two months annual bonus and at least one month performance bonus. - Colleagues are helpful and supportive. Cons - The senior management will keep chasing you when something urgent requires your help and don't even bother you at other times. - No wfh.
Some staffs act bossy and like to scold colleagues. Need to stay too long in office. Need also to involve with events and might need to stay very late.

HR from HQ is friendly, helpful & response very fast on inquiries regarding the recruitment process. As a first job for fresh graduates with no solid ideas & knowledge on the procedure. They take their time to answer my question. Kudos for that.
Great place with great people. Learnt a lot of stuff during my period. Good bosses. Challenging. High work load comes with great help from colleagues

review_title: High work load with no work life balance
review_body_text: My time at PBB was a valuable learning experience, and I'm grateful for the opportunities I had during my tenure. While there were challenges along the way, the positives outweighed the negatives. I would recommend PBB to prospective employees who are eager to work in a dynamic environment and are passionate about banking.
Can learn lot of new things and how to solve it. System part a bit outdated as time consuming is a concern. Need to have time management skills in order to complete each tasks.
Having access to quality benefits and fair compensation not only enhances job satisfaction but also contributes to the salary. Salary was higher than average.
Different business units work in silos. Hard to streamline processes to avoid manual work. However, remuneration package is good. Good for stable life.
Over all good experience and good remuneration. Challenging in the sense of multitasking and sales targets to meet. Good experience, steep learning curve.
PB offer a lot of benefits compared to any other company. This is the best thing in PB. You will think a lot of time if you want to resign because of the benefits
No work life balance, good benefits and increment with bonus. Don't expect ot claim. Eg is your ot. Great for newbie. Can learn something from pb and make it as you stepping stone
The position is really stress. Branch manager and regional manager always push you to achieve the monthly target. They keep on asking why cant achieve the target.

Our company offers exceptional benefits, including comprehensive health coverage, generous retirement plans, flexible work arrangements, professional development opportunities, wellness programs, and recognition for our hard work. These benefits reflect the company’s commitment to supporting and enriching the lives of its employees.
Salary is at least average. People who works there are energized and driven. Some employees in the IT department work in a semi-basement that doesn't see a lot of sunlight during the day.

review_title: No positive reviews, no work life balance. Everything is about selling
review_body_text: No positive reviews, no work life balance. Everything is about selling here. You don’t get commission for during cross selling. Except for clericals every other officers has to do cross selling like saving accounts and so on.

Long working hours without pay, old school, very traditional company, a lot of paperworks, system always hang especially in the evening… nothing good except the benefits like increment and bonus and low interest on HL and HP..
Expect long working hours. That being said, you get to learn a great deal about mortgages, banking and lending. Structured training is provided as well.

Im involved in HP. First of all, the position is quite hardcore. You are expected to go beyond ur KPI. The workload is quite heavy, but by time ull get used to it. The great thing about the position, is the pay, bonuses and increment. Overall its a great place to learn. But be ready to work long hours, as it is the norm here.

review_title: Not a good place unless you're willing to do anything for money
review_body_text: -No guidance given to newbies -No OT payment but expected to OT every single day -Toxic work environment, you just sit down for 9 hours and stay glued at your screen, and everybody hates it if you take a quick break to the toilet, or suggest any new approaches -Please digitalize the processes in credit as the bank is outdated in this expect, please look at other competitors.
Stress working environment High work load Need to work over time to finish the work but no pay OT Colleagues is helpful and friendly Low opportunity for career growth
Colleague are nice. But too much paperwork and need to work until 8pm everyday due to culture. Culture depend on managers. They have too much power and no one can question them.

review_title: High pay with great benefits but expect to work a lot
review_body_text: No work-life balance. Year-end bonus is very generous. 24 days of annual leave. Good place to start for fresh graduates but long working hours. Office is old.

Maybe it's just this branch do not have much OT, normally we back at 6pm. Annual leave 24 days. Colleagues are friendly, have to service with a lot of walk in customers, normally you can only start your works after 4pm

Good place to start for fresh graduates, offers a high starting salary but very long working hours. Going back at 10 pm is normal, 7 pm is the earliest time to go home, weekend sometimes need to work also, work-life balance? Don't even think about it. but offer a high salary, 24 AL, medical coverage, and a bonus.
Good for fresh grad but very stressful long working hours but good benefit and high salary if you are looking for a challange this is a good place to start

The benefits is one of the best in the industry. Staff Housing Loan, 24 days AL, Year end Bonus & Exgratia Bonus. The office is old. and not good. Have to chip in money for a Brand new water filter. :')
Chinaman company with conservative mindset. But the annual increment and bonus is good. Just the management side need to improve. The longer u work the highest bonus will get

review_title: Challenge but good for fresh graduate
review_body_text: Pros -Able to learn a lot of new things -Friendly staff -Lot of training provided -Good Management and culture Cons -Challenge for hit target, long working hrs.
Fun and enjoy environment , great to explore more finance product , can increase knowledge …… Just increament for sales staff are base on personal performance

working there was a good experience overall, but it is important to first make sure you research the branch that you are assign to so that you can get a general idea of what kind of demographic you will sell the product to.
Good to gain experience for new fresh graduate. Enjoy workplace with friendly colleague and good boss. Be able to increase skill in self independent too
Colleagues are very friendly and willing to help. But is quite stressful to hit KPI every month, branch manager will give pressure when all meeting weekly

review_title: Graduates may try which is a place to train working in pressure environment
review_body_text: What is the best part of working at the company? heavy workload and without work life balance What is the most stressful part about working at the company? No solutions when facing problems
Maybe is good to work for do not have any directions to run yourself，of course not suitable for all depends how many stressful you can handle it on you
Good salary and increment but you need to face toxic people. Staff benefits seems is good but actually nope. I think clerical is benefits most in the company compare with the officer.
Overall great place to work. Good increment, good benefits, good bonus. Promotion after 3-4 years. Health benefit also ok. Sometimes boss have favouritism.

review_title: Job security but with hard work
review_body_text: Good job security but need to able to handle the big work load. Bonuses are good with decent increment if company is profitable at the end of the year.
Good salary increment and bonus Increase knowledge and peoples skills Manage time Heavy workloads and high targets OT almost every weekdays and work on weekends
friendly and nice working environment. colleagues are helpful enough to assist and guide. direct and clear career path yet systems are bad and outdated
Need to achieve target every month. If not, then will be meeting of low performance. Always need to report what have we done in the branch and so on.
Lot to learn and good onsite training. Exposed to credit and operational at branch and HQ exposure. Lot pressure for sales but good boss (young lady) and nice teammate
Heavy workload, but good salary and benefits... Helpful and kind colleagues Stressful work environment Expected OT Iffy Career Development (Promotions) Not bad Overall
Outdated technology with school-like management. Expect workers to overtime without paying. Not environmentally friendly as everything must be printed on paper including email?!!!

review_title: Attractive benefits. And good commission payout if your sales is good
review_body_text: Unhealthy work environment, not helpful colleagues. Lack of teamwork. Good benefits have commission and bonus Both. And very flexible working environment because have an understanding Branch manager.

review_title: Typical company you can think of.
review_body_text: Worked at back office. The furniture was old and the bank rarely change the furniture. They will just fix as much as they can. The chair can be dusty and full of suspicious patches but as long as it serves the purpose, they won’t bother to improve. Working there was good in terms of learning, but at one point,if you’re not actively contributing ideas,you won’t stand out. But if you do,can be frustrated as well because the culture there(depends on department) was to promote based on seniority. The management might be the same as other company, could be frustrating dealing with them and okay at the same time. Depends on how you view the situation.

Good pay in bonus, unlimited medical benefit for staff and family, surrounded by klcc area which makes a lot of choices of eateries. Heavy workloads, no appreciation from management, unfair treatment from management team, no work life balance.

review_title: Not a good place for fresh graduates, system is outdated and many manual works
review_body_text: Try to find for other alternative instead of joint the MT program, there is no rotation for work. If you are lucky, you will meet those good and kind seniors to guide you, if not, you will be scolded for asking.

Working here is great, depends on your team. If your team is OK then your mental is OK, if not otherwise. Good salaries and benefits. Bonus is quite a lot depends on your performance and bank performance.

Public Bank has a lot of good employee benefits. The working environment is highly dependent on which branch you are working at some are great while some are less great. If you do plan on working for public bank be prepared physically and mentally.

review_title: Not the management trainee program you expect
review_body_text: Normally, management trainee program is meant to train you to become a manager. but here at public bank, it does not seem like it. i would say the job scope is relatively easy, i dont understand why they need degree holder for this job
Very Boring, as the management has require you to work overtime. Collegue are good very helpful, but too traditional minded. The environment is depend on the branch
need to work long hours, high target, lack of guidance, pay is not high, customer is not bad but most of the time you are dealing with car dealer. need to work on weekends

Generally a great place for you to jump in. Good bonuses, cheap staff loan and health benefits. Rewards loyalty. If you enjoy hectic working environment, this may be the place for you. Generally the process is still manual.
Intense and demanding workplace, often stayback post working hours but your boss will remunerate you accordingly based on your efforts and initiatives

As a compliance, you have to go through a lot of standards procedure by following the rules and regulations of Public Bank. Have to ensure that the reports are written accurately. There is a lot of OT hours to be spent too.

review_title: Stressful environment and always need to deal with high workload
review_body_text: Bad working culture, work for 12 hours per day and need to go back office on weekend&on leave. Manager will force you to go back to process case and no one is going to be your relief when you're on leave.
Require to OT to prove u are efficient. Not sure if only subject to my department. Benefit not bad, contractual bonus and good colleague ( although not all)
Hard to climb corporate ladder. Toxic environment. Normal increment. No toilet papers provided in washroom. Need to multitask and overtime. No OT provided.
Good learning curve for fresh graduates but slow career advancement. System is very manual and slow compared to other companies using latest technology

review_title: Good staff benefit with poor management
review_body_text: Management doesn't care about you. You just need to do work. Their work process keep changing. But benefits is quite good. Annual leave higher than others company.
pros_text: Unlimited(hospital warded), 6month maternity leave(3month full day, 3month half pay)
cons_text: Standby call during midnight(2 to 5 times) per month, OT claim not worth it, going back late is almost everyday(before 9pm is still considered early)

review_title: Great place to start your career especially fresh grads
review_body_text: Colleagues are helpful, manager and branch manager is supportive, depends which branch you get. Benefits and annual leaves are good. Almost perfect. You just have to be strong and adaptive to work with older generations.
Public bank is one of the bank that provides good staff benefit, including all kind of allowances, and staff loan. Inventive payout is generous too. Alot of campaigns for staff to participate in.

The positive aspects A good wage increase on top of bonuses, in addition to the standard provision of staff loans for automobiles, housing, and other specialised purposes. The obstacles to overcome Since the current boss used to be a secretary and has no prior experience in management, working hours have increased since the previous boss departed the company.

If you want higher salary than other bank for fresh grad okeyy if not don't join. Workload too much Environment quite toxic especially with the older colleagues The training is quite insufficient as u have to try to self learn a lot

review_title: Rigid corporate structure, top to down management style
review_body_text: Outdated management style. Top management inflexible when it comes to opinion from junior. End result is younger talents will likely jump ship while those who just follow orders passively will be rewarded or promoted by the top management. Whilst corporate life do not usually favor the creative minded ppl, rigid management will result in unproductive and unecessary workload at the end of the day. Benefit wise is comparable to other bank. Bonus structure is standard at 2month years end plus up to 4 month performance bonus in a year for normal office job while those in sales can expect double, triple and even more.
bad working culture, large age gap if u were allocated in the branch. Besides your job scope, have to work as a customer service officer, and also have a large proportion of administration work.
1. Good colleagues and all helpful. Can refer to them whenever in doubt 2. No proper knowledge transfer 3. Too much hierarchy 4. Too much SOP for everything
Sick management. Staff promotion depend on Manager. When you got the chance to promote, manager declined it by not lettting staff to transfer. Many lazy staff in the branch.

Everything depends on your team, learning opportunities, working culture, etc. SOP is very strict, everything requires your superior to get approval from management, for example: internet access, tools, etc.

High volumes Seniors are unwilling to teach & help. Managers - some old folks who is bad with computer (don't even know how to use excel...print out reports to check & use long ruler to check the alignment...) Gosh...

review_title: They have a very bad work life balance for any job that is in the Hire Purchase Department unless you are applying for a clerk position
review_body_text: They hope that you work more than the scheduled work hours and they do not compensate for OT, and the management (Hire Purchase Department) hate to see that their workers go back on time even if you have completed your work you still need to act busy and then go back at 8pm or 9 pm (schedule working hours are 8.45am - 5.45pm). If you think that bank benefits are good, you are wrong too, the benefits are all the same with any established companies just the extra thing is that if you work with them you will easily get loans from them, but then again they're pay is not high and the loan benefit is just basically paying back to the people who pays you to work there, and they receive your money with interest, is a win-win situation for them. You will get special housing loan rate with them but if you quit your job your housing interest will increase back to any normal people's interest rate. Bonuses and increment, bonus for HP department is minimum 2 months basic pay, but if you take those 2 months Bonuses and divided by 12 months and you calculate that you work an extra hour every single day, is not even enough to say that that bonuses paid your extra one hour (OT) you worked for that year. Is less then 1.5 times of the OT rate. But considering that they wish you to work until 8 pm, the bonus is not really a bonus anymore if you see it this way, you can be paid more if a company actually pays for OT. They have a performance bonuses is separately paid from the 2 months minimum bonus, this performance bonus can reach up to 6 months pay,only special cases will get 6 month bonuses and this employee actually hit close to 200% or more than 200% of his annual target, the mean average is people achieving their target is around 80% to 100% plus, you will only given this bonus if only you hit more than 100% of your target. Increment is based on KPI and there is no transparency on the calculations of your increments, they always says is up to management to calculate the increment.
job_title: Sales and Marketing Executive (Hire Purchase Officer)
pros_text: Any kind of loans, you as a staff will get a lower interest rate
cons_text: Bad work life balance and overall a bad experience

review_title: Facing different people everyday,solve a lot of problem
review_body_text: A lot of works,busy every day. many many guidelines to follow,attend customer needs ,make customer happy ....... Also a lots of benefits working there Medical claim n bonus

review_title: Depend on which branch and which division you are attached to
review_body_text: -The working environment and culture depends on which branch you are attached to-The salary package and benefits is good -No work-life balance -Lots of paperworks and workloads -Good training base for fresh grads
pros_text: The salary package and benefits is good
cons_text: -No work-life balance -Lots of paperworks and workloads -Good training base for fresh grads

review_body_text: Can get a lot of experiences there, learn all the banking regulations and law, is the best service in the banking industry, salary and benefits is good.

review_title: Good bonus but no work life
review_body_text: Promising bonus and work life balance all depended on your boss and colleague. Job security is high and career progression is good if your sales figures are good.
help to process loan applications, high volume of workload, somehow credit analyst need to do cross selling products, management and workplace culture need to be improve
Nothing much to learn from colleagues and repetitive work. Poor management and outdated equipment. Would not recommend if you are eager to learn something new.

review_title: Work culture depends on your division
review_body_text: Pretty much depends on your boss working style. Certain bosses tend to micromanage, so you are likely to feel more stressed and have less freedom at workplace. Overall, the people here I have dealt with are generally quite nice.
cons_text: Lack in basic infrastructure, hierarchy in workplace

review_body_text: This is not the kind of work you should do if you actually wanted to have a work-life balance. Anyway, my colleagues have been quite helpful. Appreciate!

review_title: Ok to work here but lately staffs are treated very unfairly
review_body_text: Was a haven to work in but now after retirement of previous bosses, new bosses not thinking of existing staffs career advancement but instead bringing in new staff
cons_text: Long working hours...they just want the job to be completed

review_title: High pay and great benefits but expect to work a lot
review_body_text: Go to the office early in the morning and leaving late, the job is also quite repetitive. But the benefits and pay are really good, the year-end bonus is very generous.
pros_text: Healthcare, employees benefits, pay, annual leave

review_title: Fast paced and very old school method
review_body_text: You got to really have passion before joining in banking sector. If you really wanted a work life balance kind of work, then this is not for you. Overall for me, I learned alot and I have a really supportive colleagues which make my life much more easier to cope up with all the problems.

review_title: Good place to start working with good and friendly colleagues.
review_body_text: Pros - helpful staff and seniors - good bonus - medical benefit - staff loans Cons - work environment - no work life balance . Just work - long hours work - slow promotion

working in branch as credit officer is the worst job position ever. you have to do tons of credit admin job, cross-selling, source for new loans, credit processing and more. not to mention, the sales staff in the bank (sales and marketing executive - sme) is the stupidest position ever, act like messenger and has minimal credit knowledge.
pros_text: good bonus and increment, medical claim
cons_text: no working balance, old style management

review_body_text: Great collogues there to help and assist you. Great environment. Normal working hours. Worklife balance was manageable. But the sales targets were high and stressful to achieve due to pandemic constrain.

review_title: Well rewarding if you are able to perform
review_body_text: Demanding sales and target based. You need to be ready to spend hours working with the bank. Good colleagues if you happen to be in a branch that wants to strive to perform. Old school kind of management.
If you're looking for security, this is the company you should apply for. Good salary and good benefits. Stable and comfortable. you just need to do the same things everyday.

If you want a stable and slow paced working environment, you could come here. Super conservative and manual work. Also, I would say depends on your department culture. You might have to pretend to be busy and stay back just to show that you sre “busy” sometimes else they might think you are very free. Cina man mindset.
People are good but office/team culture is too traditional and not really open minded. No work-life balance. But the medical benefits or other staff benefits are quite good.
Nice salary but stressful environment. Need to do cross SL which make sit very difficult. But good bonus. 2 months bonus min. But target difficult to reach. Marketing method too aggressive
Heavy workload Stress environment No work life balance But with good starting salary for fresh grad Good bonus basically is for your ot pay everyday

review_title: Really bad work culture. No work life balance. Good pay and bonus.
review_body_text: Everyone is working long hours. Would be great if management frequently check on the employees' mental health. Good pay and bonus, annual leave allocation is also great.
The working environment can be stressful and need to work overtime (Be prepared to work for long hours). Career progress for my specific department is kind of slow compared to other departments.
a good place for anyone who need career advancement however workplace culture is not that good at all, fast paced & work life not balance high volume of tasks
Stresssssfulllll Environment fast pace no work life balance at all damm stressfull no OT management is old school, IT is slow, too many procedure and rule to follow

Multitasking is a must working in PBB, especially based in Branch. We have to attend walk in customer, complete our daily task, picking up telephone calls at the same time. IT SYSTEM in PBB disappointing me because not every PC comes with Microsoft Office. Speaking of culture, it depends on Branches. Mine is quite OK, seniors are willing to teach.
I’ve been working here for 18 months,to be honest,Public Bank staff benefit is very good,I can enjoy bonus once or twice in a year.Other than this,management also very supportive.

Bonus is great among the banking industry. However, procedure of work may be very tedious. Long working hour, job will never be finished. Target will never achieve as management will further increase the target once you achieved it.
Most good benefits among banks Have to work hard , multitask Must do sales, is all about target and figures Stressful, challenging, have to work smart
Worked as a UT sales consultant in a branch. Basically, you're not going to survive and make money if you're placed into a small branch/not so crowded area. Commission structure changes every year.
Best place go learn for work and to work . Everyone have chance can try to work here as the place learn many thins as you can untill other job going to you
I was a junior executive and it’s been stressful and manager keep pushing for figure and no sense of belonging in the company. The salary is good if you can perform beyond and consistently
Stressful environment . Competitive environment Never work life balance Micromanagement Great benefit , but toxic working culture.
Friendly manager especially the sales manager of Sabah Region. He is very supportive and will make you feel comfortable. Friendly colleagues. Good salary
Need to Work fast and accurate Service above all Customer always right Lots of Training provided Rules and regulations to be adhered to Excellent working environments

It is a nice place to work with different cultures, very stressful environment at the same time it is fun. Good benefit and public bank berhad have never fail to pay the staff's bonus and exgratia. One of the best place i ever worked with.
Good to work in the bank. Teamwork among colleagues. Got paid for overtime when working on weekend. Benefits, good. Quite stressful. Good and caring bos
While i join PBB which is Top bank in Malaysia. Great benefit. The training also very professional, willing give the best to staff. Work in public bank also can connect with lots of customers.
- Typical freelance time - Learned about sales and closed sale - the management good - Hardest part of the job was an objection from the client - most enjoyable was the commission
Public Bank is a good company providing lots of benefits to Staff. Training provided in KL and colleagues are friendly. Work life balance is not for sales staff.

review_title: Do a typical work as a frontliner
review_body_text: I do a operation work in frontline. Im ok with this workflow . My strength is i can work under presure and working under minimum supervise . My weakness is about marketing. Im not a person can convince customer about a product.

review_title: A lot of things can be learned in this Bank. Good management and collaborative woking environment.
review_body_text: The Best Bank in Malaysia. Management of the Bank is very good. Collaborative working environment. Personal effort will lead to new knowledges and experiences in the business line. The Bank also provide excellent learning programmes for their staffs. Benefits? You can't never imagined.
a good start for fresh graduate to know how is the culture of banking line and getting in knowledge of compliance. Every kind of work is tough thus learn from it.

review_title: Ppb is easy job as a clerk
review_body_text: A typical day at PPB is pretty relax I learned how to manage my money in my drawer and learned how to make transaction how to print the receipt and FD(fixed deposit) We have to print dayend to make sure we have the correct amount off money in our drawer The most hardest part of the job is when u are not balanced and u have to find what amount of money u low on and try to find who was the customer
Amazing work place and team work. Training provided and fun training area. All the Colleagues are friendly and easy to adapt new environment. Offering a great help to new employees.
Stress and fast pace always brings improvement. After experienced working at other company hope could go back bank for next job. Appreciate I have experience in Public Bank.

Good stepping stone for graduates. Tip top benefits among all banks. Expect to work for long hours. Recognition comes not from hours of working. bright site: convenient to commute to working place, amazing colleagues.

review_title: Overall culture is to work as much as you can but Overtime is not claimable
review_body_text: Compensation and benefit is good Environment always is in fast pace and competitive. Even within same organisation. Competitive between other branches in same organisation in the same area

review_title: The best bank to work and great place
review_body_text: Enjoy every moment in the back and was an amazing journey of learning and sharing. I would recommend to anyone who is looking for a career in Banking. This bank delivers it
Very stable company and great staff benefits. However there’s no work life balance. They expect you to stay back everyday and come back during weekends.

- get to meet different types of customer everyday and attending their inquiry accordingly. - flexible management - learned to multitask - get to chance to attend training in Kuala Lumpur - sat for examination to obtain a license in order to sell and promote insurance products
Its a good place to work with but sometimes is might be stress when the boss keep on pushing you and do not have a good communication skills.The training center is good
Environment and cooperation with collegue are great.Sale department got target to achieve.Must and compulsory to achieve.Much courses and events.Done.

expecially in hire purchase department , no life balance. All decide & review by manager level. Make things no fair at all.Management never concern about staff health & never consider on staff position.
Colleagues are very good and willing to guide you. I enjoyed the people I worked with. It was a friendly and fun atmosphere. If you work hard you will be rewarded.
Working as a banker at loan department quite challenging. Nowadays you have to be as a multitasking staff. So you can covered up a different department.
profitable bank, good benefit, provide a good learning platform, opportunities to grow & learn from senior management. good reputation among local as well as overseas individual.

A good place to learn with comprehensive training provided. Training program and e-learning to keep staffs' knowledge up-to-date. A prospective organization in Malaysia and would be eventually expend in other countries.
Gain new experience , learn how to make an attachment with customer and how to communicate with customer. Need to take insurance examination before start to sell the insurance and join the company.
Need to improve the medical coverage as the medical coverage did not covers dependants of employee during probation period. The system of medical coverage also need to be improved.

review_title: If you are in a sales role, Good Luck
review_body_text: The Public bank is a good bank to invest in their share, not invest your time in it. The organization is too big, so it's hard to manage the corporate culture. If you work in a branch, sometimes the branch manager also can't even remember your name.
A lot of friendly staffs from various background working together

The company has a good working environment which involve in good colleagues and comfortable working space. The company concerned on employees' advantages which the main office provide in-house gym room.
The salary/benefits make up for the poor work-life balance. Promotions are highly performance based which is good. Performance bonuses are attractive as well.

review_title: Productive and fun place to work
review_body_text: Daily routine handling customer through inbound and outbound call.I learnt how to handle customer and how the banking system. Management very supportive and give advice to increase my productivity
Top quality work is expected and if perform well, will be recognised with good pay/bonuses. Staff benefits are very good especially medical benefits. May lack work-life balance during peak periods.

working culture here is encouraging employee to work hard and work overtime instead of work life balance. There are many things you could learn here like how to deal with senior and manager, complicated working relationship.
Good working environment but lack of family time as too much work to do takes our family bonding time. At time have to stay back to complete the jobs. Lack of time issues with family.

review_title: One of the Best Company in Malaysia
review_body_text: Overall a very good company for career advancement and to stay for a long term as the position very secured and got many helpful senior and training for a fresh graduates.
Good salary and benefit Good environment Multi tasks A lots i can learn. Systematic 5 working days. 2 mths bonus maintain Good friends. Provide courses
Good for management trainee. lots to learn. sometimes long hour. ...multi task job. Stress but manageable. Monday to friday job.Job benefit is okay. have to quit cuz i am moving to another country
Manage branch sales and financial products. Presenting and educate staff on bank's current updated products launch. Engaged with SME, Business Banking and Corporate customer to build more business.
job_title: Senior Corporate Financial Executive ( Basic Pay
cons_text: Very good place to learn and experience.

review_body_text: Organise a healthy sport activities to employees, such as dodgeball, badminton, ping pong, hiking, swimming and etc. Also, improve the relationship between employees with the help of sport
It is great to work with Public Bank if you have a good Branch Manager. Staff benefits are excellent. Contractual bonus, sufficient annual leave, good working environment.
Remuneration package among the best in the industry. Average 5 months bonus. Annual increment is very decent. Attractive staff rates for housing and car loan.

review_title: Stressful work environment but the reward/benefits given are great
review_body_text: During my tenure at Public Bank, I am part of Hire Purchase Credit Processing HP Centre. With team of 10 Officers, each of us need to process an average of 10 cases per day and was given 3 days to submit a decision whether the customer submission approved or rejected. It's very stressful as the pace of new case submission was very fast and It need me to go back home late everyday. Different department has different challenges. Like Housing Loan Department are less stressful as officer part only on customer credit evaluation. The rest will be done by panel lawyer as it involves government agencies. For Frontline Counter they are facing Queue Management System (QMS) which HQ will track how many customers are waiting for their number to be call in more than 2 minutes. But the best part in Public Bank, of course the bonus and ex-gratia. The management are good in taking care staff welfare. Medical benefit are given to staff and their families, 2 months bonus are given to all staff every year and in addition special ex-gratia based on staff performance up to 5 months and low interest rate 0% - 2% for staff housing loan. Overall, although work environment are stressful but working in Public Bank build my personality in terms of work wise fast and accurate, customer handling and evaluation, useful experience, etc
location: Public Bank Taman Molek HP Centre JB
pros_text: Medical Benefits, Staff Housing Loan, Bonus/Ex-gratia

review_body_text: Totalyy a different way of working, as this follow the Local way on culture and mix only local people and working across with State in Malaysia and understanding their Culture

I enjoy working here because i get many benefits such as spectacles allowance that can be claimed annually, year end bonuses and full medical claim. But, working as bank teller is quite challenging because you need to fulfill customer needs but at the same time you need follow guidelines. Nevertheless, a lot of things we can learn about finance.
pros_text: Cost of Living Allowance, spectacles allowance, bonuses, full medical benefit

review_body_text: a very good place, environment of work. learning how to do sales with a proper guide from senior. the top management also plays their roles in helping new employee to adapt with the work situation

review_title: place where you can learn a lot of things
review_body_text: Good exposure especially for fresh grad to start their career life. For the younger generation, they need to adapt to baby boomers culture. All its senior management are baby boomers. If they are humble to learn , they will learn a lot. How ever , if they are impatient , they will find big gap to communicate and implement the task.
cons_text: long working hours, full load of works

review_body_text: This is the first job after I graduated. I can learn the presentation skills and communication skills. Besides I can be more confident to speak with customers.

Public bank is a good company providing lots of benefits to the staffs. However, the working environment is bad as the working culture is terrible all the times. Top management and branch management unable to work as a team as the direction always different.
pros_text: Good staff benefits low rate housing loan and family medical benefits
cons_text: Bad management team with bad working culture

review_body_text: Remuneration package among the best in the industry. Average 5 months bonus. Annual increment is very decent. Attractive staff rates for housing and car loan. Work life balance is acceptable depending on which department you work at and the boss you work for. Staff dismissal is rare and unheard of except in extreme cases. Great place to work to raise a family and retire. Company prefers to hire fresh graduates than outsiders. Career progression is steady, even predictable up to a certain level.

Working in Public bank was really bring me a lot of experience. The way of communicate with problem customer and how to work in a big team. The fun of the work place is we can meet many different type of customer and colleagues.

review_title: Long working hours but love the colleagues
review_body_text: A typical day at work would be processing ad hoc appraisal reports for new recruits / internal promotions. Also includes handling annual increment, performance bonus and year-end bonus. I learned a lot about handling problematic staff and dealing with higher management people. The workplace culture is pretty traditional - your boss is always right! Hardest part of the job would have to be juggling ~10 tasks (all with the same deadlines!) At the same time, it's also the most enjoyable part of the job - the sense of satisfaction you get when you manage to complete everything on your to-do list.
pros_text: Great staff benefits, bonuses, no office politics
cons_text: Boss can be crazy during peak period

review_title: Productive and Having Fun with the colleagues
review_body_text: Try to communicate with each other from another department, they can assists us to archiving target or sales. They are more experience and has work at the banking industries for many years.
cons_text: Courses required and license exam foc, phone bill claimed max rm120

review_body_text: A good place for fresh graduates Learning of market is competitive Learning of the real working society Learning of need self discipline Learning of time management

review_title: Extra long working hours even on weekends
review_body_text: PBB is a very systematic and established company but the working hour is too long and it is compulsory to stay late everyday even you had finish your job. It is very often that you will be called in to work overtime on weekend but only able to claim half of the full overtime amount.
cons_text: very long hours, compulsory to stay late at work everyday.

review_body_text: Good place to learn new things. Programming is the essential for my position. Management very strict in term of task completion and concern what staff need. Hardest part will be to accomplish project and task given before the dateline. Friends and team will be the best thing.

review_title: Working together as a team work .
review_body_text: To provide curteous and efficiency in customer services. Done all the work assign by senior office on the same day. Be a punctual staff on working time.

Good workplace for develop skill and knowledge. Good management. Very condusive and clean place. You can discover new things here. Suitable for person eho love kids very much. Can handle stress very well.
pros_text: Free lunch, all the staff women

review_body_text: Great Company and provide good benefits for staff, but didnt plan well on staff career enhancement for certain department. Able to learn a lot knowledge and provide continue training to staff
cons_text: Long hours and have to work on weekend

review_body_text: the management are so good. but the environment depends on how good your workmates. they could influence your working surroundings. try your best to mingle with the correct person so that you will have a good motivation to go to work everyday.
I was in charge of the Bank's operational aspect. Daily movement of physical cash, accounts, staff training & annual leaves, compliances & reporting.
pros_text: Generous staff loans for housing & vehicles
cons_text: Too tied up with paper work

review_body_text: This company is very good on their benefits Medical, they have cola allowance, and also bonus every years. Daily working quite busy due to the have to achieve time for attending customer.Workplace culture was very good. The hardest part on this job is only when the bank is full waiting about 300 person before and after holiday( in seri petaling branch).Enjoyable part is when they have trip under company.

review_title: Good company in terms of remuneration but not much opportunity for career advancement
review_body_text: Run! if you are looking for career advancement place. Work environment highly dependent on which branch you are placed in. Be prepared to work hard, work late and ability to multitask is a plus point!
pros_text: Low housing loan rates; good bonus
cons_text: long working hours, career advancement almost non existent

review_title: Productive and best working place ever
review_body_text: 5 Working days. My 1st and best working experience ever for 7 years. Learned to attend and face customers directly. And to solve many management issues. Fun and productive collegues. So far I didn't find any hardest or easiest job in my experiences. Tq
i not enjoy the inviroment at my office now.the work culture is not okay today.so tired every day to travelling from my home to workplace.benefit also no satisfied to me.

review_title: Fun to work with Public Bank
review_body_text: Happy to work almost 10 years in banking line, What i learn is operation and loan department. Management is messy woth workloads. Hardest part of the job is team is not helping each other with workloads. The most enjoy thing is five working days in bank.

I like to help people. Position of a teller offers a unique chance to combine the two things in one job. I really like the customer service. It’s a good idea to pay attention to what the managers are doing and learn about things like mortgages and other loans, reading credit reports, etc. You may need a loan in the future or decide to become a homeowner someday and it’s a good idea to know what options you have and to understand the importance of good credit.

It is a best training ground for fresh graduate to develop and growth ourself with the work culture and environment. The high demand from management and high volume of job allow us to learn to manage our time well and be multitask to meet the assigned job duties dateline. The most enjoyable part of the job is having a gang of good colleagues who willing to work together and resolve problem together.

Competition among colleague is high as branch manager have total authority influencing an individual career progress. Being an credit officer, I am under pressure to balance between productivity and quality work as this would directly affect the bank's books. Nonetheless, the bank provide attractive career pathway where every employees are given opportunity to climb up the corporate ladder.

The top management is consecutive and they are not willing to expose to risk. The workplace culture is horrible which the senior will greatly utilize the young staff. The hardest part is to deal with horrible work culture which emphasize on long working hours. Nothing is enjoyable here.
Busy and hectic working place everyday but yet interesting and challenging. A good place to learn with good career advancement. High management expectations with good reward offered.

review_title: It's a good place to work
review_body_text: Culture is old school but they take care of their staff. Sometimes promotion is not easy, comes very late. It's usually a busy day at work. Bonus is good. The People are friendly

8 years in public bank has been quite overwhelming for me as a bank executive. I work in the operation department. Specifically in the cheque clearing department. I'm a verifier. I verify all the cheques we received from the branches via data everyday. We start 5 am in the morning and we finish at 2 pm in the evening which I would say the most enjoyable part of the job. This job teaches me to be very skillful in observation, decision making and working as a team.
strict and serious environment , customer service oriented , front line services , professional management , good benefits for employees , fun workplace

I working for more with the top 10 banker in Malaysia. I started from clerk until promoted to senior executive. I learn many thing from the co as I started from the bottom. I would like to change my carrier as I see I can't go better future with this co anymore.
Overall working in the bank very interesting & Challenging. Learned more banking knowledge and improved myself in planning, multiply savings & saving money

Interviewing & processing multiples loan such as housing loan, personal loan & business loan. To perform a Trade Finance Transaction such as Letter of Credit, Trust Receipt, Bankers Acceptance & Bank Guarantee. To perform frontline transaction such as deposit/withdrawal/telegraphic transfer & customer service.

review_title: Provide good benefit but a tough position for the beginner
review_body_text: Good opportunity for fresh graduate to tried but need to struggle because sales position need you to more active mostly when related in insurance product.

Public Bank is a really good company for anyone to start a career in banking with. It is a well managed company that continue to strive and excel in this ever challenging world and a company that values hard work and dedication in performing everyday task.

-very busy working life every day - I learned almost everything from public bank especially management skills and customer service and also multi-tasking. - well organised management - Asian working culture - the hardest part is too much of work and less increment. - United and teamwork

- basic daily jobs are including processing of housing loan applications & customer service - it is important to have well time management and teamwork in order to get the job done - the most enjoyable time will be the time you received your bonuses every year

review_title: appreciate for everything that i have learn
review_body_text: it is my first time to work as insurance specialist , it full of challenging and require a lot of skills. i so glad to work in bank as i learnt many banking operation and sales knowledge .
Public bank is 3rd larges bank in malaysia. Stable companies for more than 50yrs. Goods in staff benefits. Training will be provided for candidates. High paid salary.

review_title: Have some gymnasium ,and always do fizikal training
review_body_text: Good team work in team,always alert in all situation,keep eye on stranger people who make a deal with public bank,always be focus on duty ,and make sure all custumer always in safe situation in good.
It was an overall positive experience with Public Bank Berhad in terms of my job scope whereby I get to learn the essential selling skills required to perform in a sales driven position

Public bank is a typical 'chinaman' company. Employees are expected to work hard for company but not being appreciated accordingly. Labor turnover rate is high especially among 80's & 90's youth. Most of them can't accept the seniority culture applicable in the bank. Last but not least, it's still a good starting point for those fresh graduates to learn, a good place to learn the multitasking and stress handling skill.

review_title: Branch - High workload accompanied with good perks
review_body_text: Review from branch based employee 1) Life working in a branch can hardly escape unpaid OT. 2) Software for loans are rather inefficient. 3) Involves a lot of paperwork instead of computerization. 4) Bad management & culture as overly high workload is considered responsibility of staff which is true but no appreciation is noticed for time spent on unpaid OT. 5) Employees are forced to work on Saturday and Sunday without reimbursement. 6) Good bonus and nice colleagues.

Overall, I enjoyed every single day of my working day at Public Bank Berhad. This is because, my branch manager and also my colleagues are very supportive and they have helped me on how ti perform my tasks and jobs. The hardest part of my tasks was when I am the clearing department. The most interesting part when I get the chance to help people at from desk and ask on how I can help to do the transactions.

review_title: Fun work place,team work and lots of banefits
review_body_text: I have been work with public bank for 5 years.This place is so much fun eventhough there are so much work to do.Everyone have their own job and we work as a team to ensure customer satisfied with our service.and also there are a lots of banefits that i get since working with public bank such as cola,healthcare,bonus and increment salary every year.

Provides informative expertise of financial products and services to external customer in a professional manner. Undergoes training for financial products and services including personal banking, investment banking, sale and management of unit trust funds, and bancassurance. Requires ability to interpret well

Daily routine in Public Bank is to evaluate customers' profile and liaise with different branches to get more customers' information before making lending decision. I have learnt to work effectively in a fast paced working environment as there is always deadline in submitting reports. I have also learnt to communicate effectively with all the branches in Malaysia. The management of the company is very centralized and systematic. Co-workers are friendly and we helped each other while working. The most enjoyable part of the job is when challenges happens such as when customers' provided fraud documents, it is our job to detect the fraud and when there is new systems to be tested.

It is good to work in Public Bank Berhad as a senior financial executive. My position promotes bancassurance products and other financial products. I achieved 2 years consecutively Public Bank foreign incentive trip in year 2014 and 2015. However, it could be better if the company culture can be improved. Overall, I am seeking a new environment and self-growth in learning things and professionalism in the workplace.

review_title: Quite tough job however nice team members make us release the stress
review_body_text: The job scope of sales executive is not only to get a case, develop relationship networks with dealers and customers to market the hire purchase, we need to follow up each stage of the case from the day the case assign until the disbursement of the case. I have learned how to assign the case, submission to hq, processing (CCRIS, calculation of the interest rate, EIR, DSR, CRR, guarantor and so on), LOU, e-hakmilik, payment, disbursement and so on. The hardest part of the job is to build a new relationship and dealing with dealers and customers. In addition, with the current economics problem, the market was very slow, so quite tough to survive. However, the most enjoyable part of the job is when we can give the best services to our customers and make them satisfied with us.

review_title: Productive and fast banking line also much benefit.
review_body_text: So best company and benefit provided also staff have right in union for any supporter.have be responsible in each department also learn to have more knowledge in every bank product.Have to always doing and following AMLA guidelines..

Public Bank is no doubt a reputable company with good track record and success. All this will not happen without contribution from dedicated staff who strive for the success of the company. As far my long service in Public Bank, I learned a lot to be a good and responsible staff. The most important things are team spirit to ensure all the responsibilities are to be carried out successfully. The Management also is very concern and alert that all opinion and complain from the customers are taken care off. This is one of the reason why Public Bank brand name is quite acceptable to all customers nationwide. All these qualities will be a good example for me to apply when I join new company as well.
the benefit given provide by this company is good and can get many knowledge from this working experienced. All colleagues were very helpful and cooperative.
I like working environment. In this 4 years, i were grow up a lot. My relationship with colleagues like big family, we take care of each others. I like it so much.
pros_text: Take a good care of staff, staff benefits.

review_body_text: Bosses might not be as friendly as they can be, but they will know whether you are a good worker or a bad worker. They see, they evaluate, they know what is right and wrong, they do not listen to one sided only, and you get your chance fairly as to others, regardless of who you are, where you from, or what your previous academic or performance proved. In short, everyone gets a shot.
The credit application process involves few departments. Thus, great tam work learned in running the daily job. Need critical thinking in the whole process.

Remuneration package among the best in the industry. Average 5 months bonus. Annual increment is very decent. Attractive staff rates for housing and car loan. Work life balance is acceptable depending on which department you work at and the boss you work for. Staff dismissal is rare and unheard of except in extreme cases. Great place to work to raise a family and retire. Company prefers to hire fresh graduates than outsiders. Career progression is steady, even predictable up to a certain level

Being as a banker is like facing a challenge everyday and stress everyday, from the day you started your work untill the time you have finished your work, you will always be sitting on a same place, i have learn to manage my stress and to move more faster in work since my work always have a date line, management is at average rate that i could give, however my co-workers are also been always busy preparing work so have less time to talk in order to reduce our stress level inside of our body, the hardest part of the job is when your assigned to prepare more than 2 jobs which has a same date line to prepare as sson as possible, but the most enjoyable part of the job if you attend the customer, consulting them, and put yourself in their shoes

A good place for your learning process when dealing with client and complaint. Having a very humble CEO. Lots of high and low when its come to other co workers. Feel great when you accomplished a task that given to you

As it is the head office of Public Bank, there is definitely lots of paper works that need to be handled. Colleagues around us look stressful sometimes but they never have the thought of leaving the Bank, i think the reason behind this was the Bank provides them a good environment and the Bank gives many benefits to them. During the period of internship, i learnt a lot from my colleagues. The bank's working environment is comfortable and nice.
- Job is the same throughout everyday as it is routine job. - Not much chance for job advancement. - Long working hours as no one is going back on time.

review_title: Huge corporation with outstanding yearly profit showing
review_body_text: My job consist of sales and marketing of the bank loan products. I learnt much about communication and customer service skills throughout my time there. The company have a very understanding management as well as supportive co-workers. I find it difficult to hit the set target every month due to my lack of experience in the field, but eventually I improved through hardwork and dedication. What I enjoyed the most is the satisfaction from completing a done deal and getting praise and appreciation from customers.

review_title: Productive with good air conditioning in this hot weather
review_body_text: I love and enjoy my working life in Public Bank for the past 28 years and taking this opportunity to thank Public Bank. I like the management style who entrust me to handle the Bills, Remittances,Foreign Currency Current Account and Fixed Deposits, Safe Deposits Box and Chief Cashier SOLELY with only one clerical staff. Month end there are a high numbers of Telegraph Transfer , Demand Draft and Bills to draw down with cut of time. To meet the monthly Figures for loans and Non Interest Incomes. with limited Staff. Manage to increase monthly figures to reach the yearly targets by the month of September to achieve the branch yearly awards.
pros_text: 24 hours Guards start work early, attend calls and customers inquir, back logs after office hours & review work on weekends & public holidays
cons_text: Too long hours of work. health and family effected

review_title: Productive and high committed working culture
review_body_text: - Good training provided and friendly colleagues - Company that focuses on training and results oriented

review_title: Very good place to start a career. Lot of things to learn and there is always task to perform
review_body_text: Basically perform development and provide support for the Bank's Treasury system, overseas branch core banking system and offshore banking' s treasury and accounting system. Have very good knowledge on Bank's Treasury Related systems, core banking system etc. As of now involved more on managing staff's and provide consultancy, attend meeting with stakeholders. The hardest part of the job is when issues arise, providing solution and rectification work is not an issue but handling of the management is tough part. Always occupied and enjoys the constant job pressure.
pros_text: Benefit offered and always there are things to learn

review_body_text: Good and productive bank company but I have to make a decision to leave for the career that I love to do most i.e involve in Offshore Industries

review_title: Quite a conducive and friendly office environment.
review_body_text: Basically, my duties involve liaising with Public Bank's motor vehicle hirers - making telephone calls as a gentle reminder for them to make payment. In cases of default payment, we need to seek the assistance of repossessors appointed by our Company to repossess the motor vehicle from our hirer. I would say this is the hardest part of my job.
pros_text: sometimes we have pot luck party after a hard day's work.
cons_text: not allowed to take leave either beginning or month-end.
